intent: Trace POST /identity/api/auth/login. Follow the LoginForm body from AuthController.authenticateUser into UserService.authenticateUserLogin. Annotate the entrypoint and the service-layer implementation.
Trace POST /identity/api/auth/signup. Follow SignUpForm from AuthController.registerUser into UserRegistrationService.registerUser. Annotate the entrypoint and note any validation / dedup checks.

Trace POST /identity/api/auth/verify. Follow JwtTokenForm.token from AuthController.verifyJwtToken into UserService.verifyJwtToken and the JwtProvider's validate path. Identify how signed tokens are checked.

Trace GET /identity/vehicle/{carId}/location. The handler resolves any car location by UUID with no comparison against the calling user. Annotate the entrypoint and the service method it delegates to.

Trace DELETE /identity/api/v2/admin/videos/{video_id}. The handler is named to imply admin-only access yet performs no role check itself — that responsibility is delegated to the service layer. Annotate the entrypoint and the service method.

Trace GET /identity/api/v2/user/videos/convert_video. The handler reads `video_id` from the query string and calls ProfileService.convertVideo, which in the legacy code path runs a shell command built from conversion params stored on the ProfileVideo record. Annotate the entrypoint.

Trace POST /identity/api/v2/user/verify-email-token. Follow ChangeEmailForm with the verification token from ChangeEmailController.verifyEmailToken into UserService.verifyEmailToken. Note how the token is matched.

Trace POST /identity/api/v2/user/verify-phone-otp. Follow ChangePhoneForm with the OTP from ChangePhoneController.verifyPhoneOTP into UserService.verifyPhoneOTP. Note whether attempt counting / rate-limit guards the check.
